team.name: executive-team
team.header: Team for discussing executive matters and strategic planning
team.purpose: To provide a dedicated space for strategic decision making and planning for MediConn's executives

team.name: customer-support-team
team.header: Team for discussing customer support issues, strategies, and improvements
team.purpose: To provide a dedicated space for customer support related conversations

channel.name: strategic_planning
channel.header: Channel for discussing and planning strategic initiatives
channel.purpose: To discuss and plan strategic initiatives to improve MediConn's services

channel.name: customer_issues
channel.header: Channel for discussing and resolving customer issues
channel.purpose: To discuss and resolve issues faced by MediConn's customers

post.message: Just to confirm, our next strategic planning meeting is scheduled for next Tuesday at 10AM, correct?
Team, we've been receiving positive feedback on our new user interface. It seems to be more intuitive for the patients.
Just a reminder that we need to finalize the Q2 budget by the end of this week. Please review the proposal and provide your feedback.
We need to schedule a training session for the new software next week. John, could you coordinate this?
We have been invited to participate in the Virtual Healthcare Conference next month. Who should we nominate to represent MediConn?

I've been noticing a recurring confusion among patients. When a virtual doctor recommends a patient to see a specialist or get lab work done, 31% of these patients contact support, unsure if MediConn coordinates these external appointments or if they have to do it themselves.

Thanks for bringing this up, John. We need to clarify this process for our patients. It seems our platform can generate referrals and requisitions, but the booking for these external services is something the patients have to do independently. This confusion is leading to an increase in service calls. Let's brainstorm some solutions in our next team meeting.

We have a customer communication issue that needs to be addressed. Based on the feedback we are getting from the support team, patients are confused about the process of booking appointments with external specialists or labs after a virtual doctor's recommendation. This confusion is causing a significant increase in service calls. We need to improve our communication regarding this aspect to reduce the volume of calls. Looking for strategic ideas on how we can address this.
